mnemonics.title: Collision Resolution Techniques
mnemonics.content: CHAINING KEEPS ELEMENTS IN LINKED LISTS; OPEN ADDRESSING FINDS NEW SPOTS (Linear, Quadratic, Double)

mnemonics.title: Open Addressing Probes
mnemonics.content: LINEAR CHECKS NEIGHBORS; QUADRATIC USES SQUARES; DOUBLE HASHING PUNTS (to secondary)

mnemonics.title: Load Factor Trigger
mnemonics.content: SEVENTY FIVE PERCENT - REHASH BEFORE YOU CRUMBLE

acronyms.usage: Hash tables provide FAST data retrieval through mathematical address calculation

acronyms.expansion: Last In First Out - used in probe sequences
acronyms.usage: Linear probing revisits previous positions when searching
A popular hash function using string character accumulation with multiplication

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a LARGE PARKING LOT (hash table) with numbered spots. Each car (data item) gets assigned a parking spot number by the hash function (parking attendant). Multiple cars wanting the same spot must wait in a line (chaining) or find another spot (open addressing).
visualTips.purpose: Understanding hash table as an array of buckets with collision handling

visualTips.visualization: Think of collision resolution like finding a seat in a crowded theater: LINEAR PROBING means checking the next seat; QUADRATIC PROBING skips by 1, 4, 9 seats; DOUBLE HASHING asks another usher for the next row to try.
visualTips.purpose: Visualizing different probing strategies for handling collisions

visualTips.visualization: Like a LIBRARY CATALOG system: each book (key) gets a unique code (hash) that tells you exactly which shelf and position to find it, without searching through the entire library.
visualTips.purpose: Understanding how hash functions provide direct access to data

analogies.analogy: Think of a dictionary where words are arranged alphabetically - you don't search page by page, you use the letter index to jump directly to the right page. Hashing does the same but with numerical addresses.

Like two people having the same locker number in a school - the school needs a rule to handle this (either give one a different locker or let both share). Hash tables use collision resolution techniques for this.

Like a bus becoming more crowded - with few passengers (low load factor), finding a seat is easy (fast). As more people board (high load factor), finding seats becomes harder and takes longer (slower operations).
Like moving to a bigger house when your family grows - you take everything from your old house and redistribute it in the new, larger space to live comfortably again.
Like each apartment building (bucket) can have multiple apartments (chain of elements). Even if 10 people want to live in Building 5, they can all fit in different units of that building.
